source.title: Suspected neurological conditions: recognition and referral
source.population: Adults aged over 16 years with suspected neurological conditions.
source.setting: Initial assessment and referral from primary or community care.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected adult dizziness, vertigo, limb weakness, sensory symptoms, and tremor referral features; not applicable to children and not directly validated for UAE pathways.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.7.1–1.7.10 and 1.10.1–1.10.6 — limb weakness and sensory symptoms

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports sudden, progressive, fluctuating, unilateral, symmetrical, sensory-associated, and back-pain-associated weakness, urgent referral, and clinician-led imaging where specified.

items.rationale: Exact phrase located in inspected full source.

items.final_wording: Supports sudden, progressive, fluctuating, unilateral, symmetrical, sensory-associated, and back-pain-associated weakness, urgent referral, and clinician-led imaging where specified.

source.title: Suspected neurological conditions: recognition and referral

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.7.1–1.7.10 and 1.10.1–1.10.6 — limb weakness and sensory symptoms

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports sudden, progressive, fluctuating, unilateral, symmetrical, sensory-associated, and back-pain-associated weakness, urgent referral, and clinician-led imaging where specified.

items.rationale: The exact adult weakness section supports documenting sudden, progressive, fluctuating, unilateral, symmetrical, sensory-associated, and back-pain-associated weakness plus functional impact without assigning a neurological diagnosis. The exact section supports clinician-assessed focal neurology, gait, imbalance, bowel or bladder, systemic concern, existing results, referral status, and follow-up without inferring examination or urgency.
